violations.text: Milk was observed held at 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025
Milk Cooler located in the 5/6 area was not in good repair, ambient observed at 53F. PIC took unit out of service at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

Chicken patty sandwiches and spicy chicken sandwiches were observed at 110-131 F in the hot box unit in cafeteria 7&8. The ambient air temperature was observed at 110 F. Hot box unit was accidently turned off. Hot box unit was turned on and reheated to 170 F. Chicken patty sandwiches and spicy chicken sandwiches were reheated to 205 F during time of inspection. Food must be hot held at 135 F or higher. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Chicken patty sandwiches and spicy chicken sandwiches were observed held at 117-126 F in the steam table of cafeteria 7&8. Hot food must be held at 135 F and above. Establishment replaced sandwiches with sandwiches that were 135 F and above during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Chicken patty sandwiches and spicy chicken sandwiches were observed held at 116-120 F in the steam table of cafeteria 5&6. Hot food must be held at 135 F or above. Employee was observed assembling sandwiches on a cart. Sandwiches must hot held. Sandwiches were placed back in the hot-hold unit and reheated. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Food was observed held at 44-45 F in the salad bar ice well unit in cafeteria 7&8. Food must be held at 41 F or below. Per person in charge, food had been in the unit for about 50 minutes. Food was relocated to a refrigeration unit to cool. If establishment can not hold food at 41 F or below, establishment can not use salad bar ice well. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Food was observed held at 48-56 F in the salad bar ice well unit in cafeteria 5&6. Food must be held at 41 F or below. Per person in charge, food had been in the unit for about 50 minutes. Food was relocated to a refrigeration unit to cool. If establishment can not hold food at 41 F or below, establishment can not use salad bar ice well. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Food was observed held at 122-133 degrees Fahrenheit in the steam tables and hot cabinet of cafeteria grade 5&6 and 7&8. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the hot hold units for about 1.5 hours. Food was reheated and the temperature of the units were turned up. Establishment must not use the hot hold units if they can not maintain an internal food temperature of 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above. Temperatures must be monitored. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Food was observed held at 47-58 degrees Fahrenheit in the salad bar unit and on the counter in the cafeteria of grades 5&6. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the unit and on the counter for about 1.5 hours. Food was cooled down during time of inspection. Establishment must not store food on the counter. Establishment must not use salad bar if it can not maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Temperatures must be monitored. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Popcorn chicken salad was observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit in the cold-hold buffet unit in cafeteria 5-6. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been out of temperature for approximately 1.5 hours. Food was relocated to the refrigerator to cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Food was observed held at 48-52 degrees Fahrenheit is the salad bar in cafeteria 5-6. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been out of temperature for no longer than 1.5 hours. Food was relocated to the refrigerator to cool. Establishment must use more ice packs and keep food covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Food was observed held at 44-50 degrees Fahrenheit is the salad bar in cafeteria 7-8. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been out of temperature for no longer than 1.5 hours. Food was relocated to the refrigerator to cool. Establishment must use more ice packs and keep food covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
